name: libtime-moment-perl
title: libtime-moment-perl
summary: Perl C/XS module representing date and time of day with UTC offset
description:
Perl C/XS module representing date and time of day with UTC offset
Time::Moment is an immutable object representing a date and time of day with
an offset from UTC in the ISO 8601 calendar system.
.
Time is measured in nanoseconds since 0001-01-01T00Z. Leap seconds are
ignored.  Time::Moment can represent all epoch integers from -62,135,596,800
to 2,534,02,300,799; this range suffices to measure times to nanosecond
precision for any instant that is within 0001-01-01T00:00:00Z to
9999-12-31T23:59:59Z.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use libtime-moment-perl because Perl C/XS module representing date and time of day with UTC offset.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use libtime-moment-perl as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair libtime-moment-perl with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `libtime-moment-perl --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
